records.ety: [Gr. dia`tomos cut in two. See Diatomous.]

defs: One of the Diatomaceæ, a family of minute unicellular Algæ having a siliceous covering of great delicacy, each individual multiplying by spontaneous division. By some authors diatoms are called Bacillariæ, but this word is not in general use.
A particle or atom endowed with the vital principle.
quotes: The individual is nothing. He is no more than the diatom, the bit of protoplasm.
